questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空根据yet（已经/还）可知用现在完成时Have...finished；第二空根据an hour ago（一小时前）可知用一般过去时finished。A和D的第一空时态错误；C的第二空时态错误，ago是一般过去时的标志词，不能与现在完成时连用。【技巧】记住时间标志词：yet/already/since+现在完成时；yesterday/ago/last year+一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ is talking with our teacher is Bosco's sister.
questions.explanation: 【解析】先行词是the girl（人），定语从句中缺少主语，所以用who。B which指物；C whom指人但作宾语；D whose表示'……的'，在从句中作定语。这里需要主格形式who。【技巧】定语从句选择关系词口诀：'人物who/that，物which/that，人物宾语whom，whose表所属'。判断方法：先找先行词是人还是物，再看从句缺什么成分。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The bridge ______ by the workers last year.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据last year（去年）可知用一般过去时；bridge（桥）是被建造，所以用被动语态。一般过去时被动语态结构：was/were + 过去分词。主语bridge是单数，所以用was built。A是主动语态一般现在时；B是主动语态一般过去时；D是一般现在时被动语态，时态错误。【技巧】被动语态判断：'主语是动作的承受者'，结构'be+done'，根据时间词确定be的时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海了，还没回来）可知'不可能是他'。can't表示'不可能'，用于否定推测。must be表示'一定是'（肯定推测）；might be表示'可能是'（不确定）；can通常不用于肯定推测句中。【技巧】情态动词表推测：must（一定，肯定），can't（不可能，否定），might/may（可能，不确定）。根据语境逻辑判断确定程度。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you don't know the word, you can ______ in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: 【解析】look up表示'查阅（词典、资料）'，代词it要放在中间，即look it up。look for表示'寻找'；look over表示'检查'。B选项代词位置错误；C和D语义不符。【技巧】'动词+副词'短语中，代词作宾语必须放中间（如pick it up, turn it on），名词可放中间或后面（look the word up / look up the word）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据'covered with snow all year round'（终年积雪）可知是山的'top'（山顶）。foot/bottom指山脚，通常不会终年积雪；side指山腰，也不符合'终年积雪'的常识。只有山顶符合语境。【技巧】词义猜测要结合常识和语境线索，注意地理常识：海拔越高气温越低，积雪常在山顶。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The news made everyone ______. They all felt very ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空修饰news（物），用exciting（令人兴奋的）；第二空修饰they（人），用excited（感到兴奋的）。-ing形容词修饰物，表示'令人……的'；-ed形容词修饰人，表示'感到……的'。【技巧】口诀：'形物ING，感人ED'。即形容物用-ing形式（interesting/book），形容人用-ed形式（interested/person）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据下文'They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste'（他们常把可回收纸放进厨余垃圾桶）可知，一开始学生觉得垃圾分类很'难'。A容易的、C有趣的、D可能的，均与下文逻辑不符。【技巧】完形填空要利用下文线索，通过具体行为反推形容词含义。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school carried out a waste sorting activity. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

At first, many students found it (8)______ to tell the difference between different types of waste. They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste. However, with the help of teachers and volunteers, things started to (9)______.

Bosco, a student from Class 3, created a special poster. It used different colors to show which bin each type of waste should go into. "I wanted to make waste sorting (10)______ for everyone," he said. His idea worked well. More and more students began to sort their waste (11)______.

By the end of the month, the amount of waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%. "Small actions can make a big (12)______," said the headmaster. "If we all do our part, we can create a greener future."

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据However（然而）转折和下文'His idea worked well'可知，情况开始'改变'。A消失、C停止、D继续，均不符合语境。change表示从混乱到有序的改善。【技巧】注意转折词However/But，前后文意相反，前文讲困难，后文讲好转。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school carried out a waste sorting activity. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

At first, many students found it (8)______ to tell the difference between different types of waste. They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste. However, with the help of teachers and volunteers, things started to (9)______.

Bosco, a student from Class 3, created a special poster. It used different colors to show which bin each type of waste should go into. "I wanted to make waste sorting (10)______ for everyone," he said. His idea worked well. More and more students began to sort their waste (11)______.

By the end of the month, the amount of waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%. "Small actions can make a big (12)______," said the headmaster. "If we all do our part, we can create a greener future."

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据上文'created a special poster...used different colors to show'可知，Bosco制作海报是为了让垃圾分类变'容易'。A困难的、B无聊的、D无用的，均与文意相反。【技巧】理解目的关系，制作辅助工具的目的是简化事情，选与'difficult'相反的词。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school carried out a waste sorting activity. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

At first, many students found it (8)______ to tell the difference between different types of waste. They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste. However, with the help of teachers and volunteers, things started to (9)______.

Bosco, a student from Class 3, created a special poster. It used different colors to show which bin each type of waste should go into. "I wanted to make waste sorting (10)______ for everyone," he said. His idea worked well. More and more students began to sort their waste (11)______.

By the end of the month, the amount of waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%. "Small actions can make a big (12)______," said the headmaster. "If we all do our part, we can create a greener future."

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据上文'His idea worked well'和下文'waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%'可知，学生们开始'正确地'分类垃圾。quickly快速地、slowly慢慢地、happily开心地，都不如correctly准确地体现垃圾分类的改善效果。【技巧】注意上下文呼应，后文数据改善证明分类'正确性'提升。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school carried out a waste sorting activity. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

At first, many students found it (8)______ to tell the difference between different types of waste. They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste. However, with the help of teachers and volunteers, things started to (9)______.

Bosco, a student from Class 3, created a special poster. It used different colors to show which bin each type of waste should go into. "I wanted to make waste sorting (10)______ for everyone," he said. His idea worked well. More and more students began to sort their waste (11)______.

By the end of the month, the amount of waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%. "Small actions can make a big (12)______," said the headmaster. "If we all do our part, we can create a greener future."

questions.explanation: 【解析】固定搭配make a difference表示'有影响、起作用'。句意：小行动可以产生大影响。make a decision做决定；make progress取得进步（不与a连用）；make a mistake犯错误，均不符合句意。【技巧】记住中考常考固定搭配：make a difference（有影响），take action（采取行动），play a role（起作用）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school carried out a waste sorting activity. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of environmental protection.

At first, many students found it (8)______ to tell the difference between different types of waste. They often put recyclable paper into the bin for kitchen waste. However, with the help of teachers and volunteers, things started to (9)______.

Bosco, a student from Class 3, created a special poster. It used different colors to show which bin each type of waste should go into. "I wanted to make waste sorting (10)______ for everyone," he said. His idea worked well. More and more students began to sort their waste (11)______.

By the end of the month, the amount of waste sent to landfill was reduced by 40%. "Small actions can make a big (12)______," said the headmaster. "If we all do our part, we can create a greener future."

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers spend on screens daily according to the study?
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第一段'A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day'可知答案是7小时22分钟。A选项时间不足；C选项是'感到焦虑'的临界值；D选项是专家建议的娱乐屏幕时间限制。【技巧】细节题要精准定位原文，注意数字细节，不要凭印象选近似值。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs.

Dr. Li, a health expert, explains that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "When teenagers stare at screens for too long, they often forget to blink. This causes dry eyes and headaches," she says. Moreover, sitting for hours reduces physical activity, which may lead to weight gain.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests using the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Schools are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" lunch periods to encourage face-to-face communication. "We want students to build real relationships, not just virtual ones," says Mr. Zhang, a teacher.

The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 10 hours daily on screens are more likely to feel anxious. Experts recommend limiting recreational screen time to 2 hours per day and spending more time on outdoor activities.

questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. All screen time is harmful to teenagers.
B. Schools should ban smartphones completely.
C. Teenagers need to balance screen time and outdoor activities.
D. Dr. Li thinks educational apps are useless.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第三段'The key is to find a balance'和最后一段'spending more time on outdoor activities'可推断出青少年需要平衡屏幕时间和户外活动。A与第三段'not all screen time is harmful'矛盾；B文中未提及'完全禁止'；D与'educational apps...can help students learn better'矛盾。【技巧】推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断或与原文矛盾，注意转折词However后的内容常是重点。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs.

Dr. Li, a health expert, explains that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "When teenagers stare at screens for too long, they often forget to blink. This causes dry eyes and headaches," she says. Moreover, sitting for hours reduces physical activity, which may lead to weight gain.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests using the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Schools are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" lunch periods to encourage face-to-face communication. "We want students to build real relationships, not just virtual ones," says Mr. Zhang, a teacher.

The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 10 hours daily on screens are more likely to feel anxious. Experts recommend limiting recreational screen time to 2 hours per day and spending more time on outdoor activities.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 2 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据下文'When teenagers stare at screens for too long...causes dry eyes'可知'excessive screen time'指的是'过长的/过多的'屏幕时间。proper适当的；limited有限的；necessary必要的，均与'导致健康问题'的语境不符。【技巧】词义猜测利用因果关系和举例线索，后文'too long'和具体危害（dry eyes, headaches）提示该词意为'过多的'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs.

Dr. Li, a health expert, explains that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "When teenagers stare at screens for too long, they often forget to blink. This causes dry eyes and headaches," she says. Moreover, sitting for hours reduces physical activity, which may lead to weight gain.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests using the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Schools are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" lunch periods to encourage face-to-face communication. "We want students to build real relationships, not just virtual ones," says Mr. Zhang, a teacher.

The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 10 hours daily on screens are more likely to feel anxious. Experts recommend limiting recreational screen time to 2 hours per day and spending more time on outdoor activities.

questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. How to Use Educational Apps
D. The Importance of Outdoor Activities
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章围绕青少年屏幕使用时间展开，讨论了现状、危害、合理使用建议及学校措施。A只涉及手机历史，片面；B只涉及教育应用，片面；D只涉及户外活动，偏离主题。C选项全面概括了文章核心话题。【技巧】主旨题要抓首尾段和各段首句，找高频词（screen time/teenagers），排除片面选项，选择能涵盖全文的标题。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, and TVs.

Dr. Li, a health expert, explains that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "When teenagers stare at screens for too long, they often forget to blink. This causes dry eyes and headaches," she says. Moreover, sitting for hours reduces physical activity, which may lead to weight gain.

However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational apps and online courses can help students learn better. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests using the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.

Schools are also taking action. Some have started "screen-free" lunch periods to encourage face-to-face communication. "We want students to build real relationships, not just virtual ones," says Mr. Zhang, a teacher.

The study also found that teenagers who spend more than 10 hours daily on screens are more likely to feel anxious. Experts recommend limiting recreational screen time to 2 hours per day and spending more time on outdoor activities.

options: A. First, let's start with daily habits.
D. Finally, remember that teamwork makes a difference.
E. For example, set your air conditioner to 26°C in summer.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空。根据下文'Turn off lights...Unplug electronic devices'具体例子可知，此处是总分结构，需要总起句'首先，从日常习惯开始'。B另一方面（表转折）、C此外（表递进）、D最后（表总结）、E例如（表举例，但内容具体不匹配）均不合适。【技巧】注意段落结构，后文列举具体行为（关灯、拔插头）是对habits的举例说明，且下文有'When it comes to...'和'Finally'提示这是第一点。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Saving energy is important for protecting our environment. Here are some simple ways to reduce energy use at home.

(17)______. Turn off lights when you leave a room. Unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. These small habits can save a lot of electricity over time.

(18)______, try to use natural light during the day. Open curtains and blinds to let sunlight in. This not only saves electricity but also creates a pleasant atmosphere.

When it comes to heating and cooling, small changes matter too. (19)______. In winter, wear warm clothes instead of turning up the heater. In summer, use fans instead of air conditioners when possible.

(20)______. Share these tips with your family and friends. The more people join in, the greater impact we will have on our planet.

options: A. First, let's start with daily habits.
D. Finally, remember that teamwork makes a difference.
E. For example, set your air conditioner to 26°C in summer.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空。前文讲了'关灯、拔插头'，本段讲'利用自然光'，是并列的补充建议，用In addition（此外/另外）连接。A第一（已用过）、B另一方面（通常表转折对比）、D最后（段序不符）、E例如（此空后是完整句子，不是举例）。【技巧】In addition/Furthermore/Moreover用于并列补充同类观点，体现逻辑递进关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Saving energy is important for protecting our environment. Here are some simple ways to reduce energy use at home.

(17)______. Turn off lights when you leave a room. Unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. These small habits can save a lot of electricity over time.

(18)______, try to use natural light during the day. Open curtains and blinds to let sunlight in. This not only saves electricity but also creates a pleasant atmosphere.

When it comes to heating and cooling, small changes matter too. (19)______. In winter, wear warm clothes instead of turning up the heater. In summer, use fans instead of air conditioners when possible.

(20)______. Share these tips with your family and friends. The more people join in, the greater impact we will have on our planet.

options: A. First, let's start with daily habits.
D. Finally, remember that teamwork makes a difference.
E. For example, set your air conditioner to 26°C in summer.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空。本段主题是'heating and cooling'（制冷制热），空后讲冬天和夏天的做法，所以此处需要具体例子'E. 例如，夏天把空调设为26度'来承接主题。其他选项与制冷制热主题无关。【技巧】For example后通常接具体例子，验证：空调温度正是heating and cooling的具体体现。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Saving energy is important for protecting our environment. Here are some simple ways to reduce energy use at home.

(17)______. Turn off lights when you leave a room. Unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. These small habits can save a lot of electricity over time.

(18)______, try to use natural light during the day. Open curtains and blinds to let sunlight in. This not only saves electricity but also creates a pleasant atmosphere.

When it comes to heating and cooling, small changes matter too. (19)______. In winter, wear warm clothes instead of turning up the heater. In summer, use fans instead of air conditioners when possible.

(20)______. Share these tips with your family and friends. The more people join in, the greater impact we will have on our planet.

options: A. First, let's start with daily habits.
D. Finally, remember that teamwork makes a difference.
E. For example, set your air conditioner to 26°C in summer.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句填空。根据后文'Share these tips with your family and friends'（与他人分享）可知，此处强调团队合作的重要性，用Finally（最后）总结并呼吁行动。A、B、C逻辑顺序不符；E是具体例子，不适合总结段。【技巧】Finally通常用于列举最后一点或总结，后文的'The more people join in'（越多人参与）呼应了teamwork。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Saving energy is important for protecting our environment. Here are some simple ways to reduce energy use at home.

(17)______. Turn off lights when you leave a room. Unplug electronic devices when they are not in use. These small habits can save a lot of electricity over time.

(18)______, try to use natural light during the day. Open curtains and blinds to let sunlight in. This not only saves electricity but also creates a pleasant atmosphere.

When it comes to heating and cooling, small changes matter too. (19)______. In winter, wear warm clothes instead of turning up the heater. In summer, use fans instead of air conditioners when possible.

(20)______. Share these tips with your family and friends. The more people join in, the greater impact we will have on our planet.
